compiled_state.title: First stage-boundary validator at /dso:preplanning with depth-agnostic forward-compat contract and pinned self-verification semantics
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Scope
Install the first of five stage-boundary validators at /dso:preplanning, establish the depth-agnostic validator contract reused by all downstream validators, and pin the SC11 self-verification semantics.

## Done Definitions
- /dso:preplanning reads upstream PRECONDITIONS (brainstorm event) at entry; blocks with clear diagnostic if absent or schema-invalid.
- /dso:preplanning emits its own PRECONDITIONS event at exit via Story 1 writer.
- **Depth-agnostic contract pinned**: validator reads only fields defined at minimal tier; unknown fields (future standard/deep additions) are ignored, not rejected. This contract is reused by Story 4's 4 validators.
- **Self-verification semantics pinned**: (a) schema-roundtrip self-check — parse just-written event against schema before marking exit complete; (b) cross-stage chain verification — own event references upstream event's event_id; (c) brainstorm is chain-rooted via Story 1's schema-only writer check (brainstorm has no validator, so preplanning's upstream is schema-verified at Story 1 write time, not validator time).
- Zero user-interaction (SC7): all decisions derived from manifest + config; no prompts.
- Unit tests: validator accepts well-formed minimal event; rejects schema-invalid; tolerates unknown fields.
- Integration test: brainstorm → preplanning end-to-end walking skeleton.

## Considerations
- [Maintainability] This validator's code pattern is the template for Story 4's four validators — keep it depth-agnostic, zero-interactive, and schema-roundtrip-terminated.
- [Testing] Self-verification must not create bootstrap regress: the chain-of-trust roots at the brainstorm writer's schema check, not at preplanning's validator.

## Walking Skeleton
Yes — completes the brainstorm-write → preplanning-read-validate end-to-end slice started in Story 1.
